title: OutreachRequest
description: A request for DHS street outreach — reporting a person experiencing unsheltered homelessness so a mobile outreach team (StreetSmart / the borough outreach providers) can respond. NET-NEW: there is NO public DHS API and NO Open Data twin for this transaction; today a New Yorker can only report someone on the street by calling NYC311 ('Homeless Person Assistance') or 911 for an emergency. This schema serves both the read surface (list/get a reporter's own submitted requests) and the write surface (submit a new outreach request).

requestId.description: Server-assigned outreach request / NYC311 service-request number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Lifecycle status, set by DHS / the outreach provider, not the reporter.

locationDescription.description: Where the person is — nearest address, cross streets, subway station, park, or landmark.

peopleCount.description: Approximate number of people at the location.

condition.description: Observed condition of the person — helps triage the response.

emergency.description: Set true for life-safety emergencies (unresponsive, medical, extreme cold/heat). Emergencies should also be reported to 911; DHS routes non-emergency reports to the borough outreach team.

details.description: Free-text description of the situation and any relevant details.

reporter.description: Optional reporter contact details (a report can be submitted anonymously).
